requirementCueLines: Undergraduate Degree Requirements | UW William E. Boeing Department of Aeronautics & Astronautics
To earn a Bachelor of Science in Aeronautical & Astronautical Engineering, a student must complete the requirements listed below. A minimum of 180 credits is required to complete the degree.
Current A&A undergraduate students are able to access detailed information about degree requirements, deadlines, and policies on the A&A Undergraduate Student Resource Canvas.
MATH 124 Calculus with Analytic Geometry I (5)
MATH 125 Calculus with Analytic Geometry II (5)
MATH 126 Calculus with Analytic Geometry III (5)
MATH 207 (formerly MATH 307) Introduction to Differential Equations (4)
MATH 208 (formerly MATH 308) Matrix Algebra with Applications (4)
CSE 160* Data Programming (Python) or ME 123 Introduction to Visualization and Computer-Aided Design (Solidworks) (5) or other Natural Sciences - NSc (5)
CEE 220 Introduction to Mechanics of Materials (4)
9 additional writing credits are built into A&A core courses - W
5 additional diversity credits are required and can overlap with other areas of inquiry requirements - DIV
A A 310 Orbital and Space Flight Mechanics (4)
A A 321 Aerospace Laboratory I (3) - W
A A 322 Aerospace Laboratory II (3) - W
A A 447 Control in Aerospace Systems (4)
A A 420 Spacecraft and Space Systems Design I (4)
A A 421 Spacecraft and Space Systems Design II (4)
A A 405 Introduction to Aerospace Plasmas (3)
A A 430 Finite Element Analysis I (3)
A A 448 Control Systems Sensors and Actuators (3)
A A 516 Stability and Control of Flight Vehicles (3)
A A 532 Mechanics of Composite Materials (3)
A A 498 Special Topics (may only be used when pre-approved by the department)
* Up to 6 credits combined from A A 499 and ENGR 321 may be applied toward technical electives (e.g., 3 credits of A A 499 + 3 credits of ENGR 321, or up to 6 credits of either course)
Additional coursework in any subject area not used elsewhere in degree.
